---
title: "Customer Lifetime Value Analysis"
author: "Analytics Team"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width = 10)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
```

## Overview

This report analyses Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) using a cohort-based RFM
(Recency, Frequency, Monetary) model across 24 months of transaction data.

```{r load-data}
set.seed(42)
n_customers <- 1200

transactions <- tibble(
  customer_id  = sample(1:n_customers, 15000, replace = TRUE),
  order_date   = sample(seq(as.Date("2022-01-01"), as.Date("2023-12-31"), by = "day"),
                        15000, replace = TRUE),
  order_value  = rgamma(15000, shape = 2, rate = 0.01),
  product_cat  = sample(c("Electronics", "Clothing", "Home", "Sports", "Books"),
                        15000, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.25, 0.2, 0.15, 0.1)),
  channel      = sample(c("web", "mobile", "store"), 15000, replace = TRUE,
                        prob = c(0.5, 0.35, 0.15))
)

customers <- tibble(
  customer_id    = 1:n_customers,
  signup_date    = sample(seq(as.Date("2020-01-01"), as.Date("2022-06-30"), by = "day"),
                          n_customers, replace = TRUE),
  segment        = sample(c("Enterprise", "SMB", "Consumer"), n_customers,
                          replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.6)),
  acquisition_ch = sample(c("organic", "paid_search", "social", "referral"),
                          n_customers, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1))
)
```

## RFM Segmentation

```{r rfm-model}
reference_date <- as.Date("2024-01-01")

rfm <- transactions %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(
    recency   = as.numeric(reference_date - max(order_date)),
    frequency = n(),
    monetary  = sum(order_value),
    avg_order = mean(order_value),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    r_score = ntile(desc(recency),  5),
    f_score = ntile(frequency,      5),
    m_score = ntile(monetary,       5),
    rfm_score = r_score * 100 + f_score * 10 + m_score
  ) %>%
  mutate(segment = case_when(
    r_score >= 4 & f_score >= 4              ~ "Champions",
    r_score >= 3 & f_score >= 3              ~ "Loyal Customers",
    r_score >= 4 & f_score <= 2              ~ "Recent Customers",
    r_score <= 2 & f_score >= 3              ~ "At Risk",
    r_score <= 2 & f_score <= 2              ~ "Lost",
    TRUE                                     ~ "Potential Loyalists"
  ))

cat("RFM segments:\n")
print(count(rfm, segment, sort = TRUE))
```

## Lifetime Value Model

```{r clv-model}
# Merge RFM with customer metadata
clv_data <- rfm %>%
  left_join(customers, by = "customer_id") %>%
  mutate(
    tenure_days  = as.numeric(reference_date - signup_date),
    tenure_years = tenure_days / 365.25,
    predicted_clv = monetary * (frequency / tenure_years) *
                    ifelse(segment == "Champions", 3,
                    ifelse(segment == "Loyal Customers", 2.5,
                    ifelse(segment == "Potential Loyalists", 2, 1)))
  )

# Model: CLV ~ RFM scores + tenure + acquisition channel
model <- lm(log1p(predicted_clv) ~ r_score + f_score + m_score +
              tenure_years + acquisition_ch + segment,
            data = clv_data)

model_summary <- tidy(model) %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(estimate)))

cat("Significant predictors of CLV:\n")
print(model_summary, n = 10)
```

## Revenue Concentration

```{r pareto}
revenue_by_customer <- transactions %>%
  group_by(customer_id) %>%
  summarise(total_revenue = sum(order_value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_revenue)) %>%
  mutate(
    cumulative_rev = cumsum(total_revenue),
    cumulative_pct = cumulative_rev / sum(total_revenue),
    customer_pct   = row_number() / n()
  )

top_20_pct <- revenue_by_customer %>%
  filter(customer_pct <= 0.20) %>%
  summarise(revenue_share = max(cumulative_pct))

cat(sprintf("Top 20%% of customers drive %.1f%% of revenue\n",
            top_20_pct$revenue_share * 100))
```

## Visualisations

```{r plots, fig.height=8}
p1 <- rfm %>%
  count(segment) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(segment, n), y = n, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "Customer Distribution by RFM Segment",
       x = NULL, y = "Customers") +
  theme_minimal()

p2 <- clv_data %>%
  filter(predicted_clv < quantile(predicted_clv, 0.95)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = tenure_years, y = predicted_clv, colour = segment)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(title = "Predicted CLV vs Customer Tenure",
       x = "Tenure (years)", y = "Predicted CLV") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)
print(p2)
```

## Recommendations

```{r recommendations}
segment_stats <- clv_data %>%
  group_by(segment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_customers    = n(),
    avg_clv        = mean(predicted_clv),
    total_clv      = sum(predicted_clv),
    avg_frequency  = mean(frequency),
    avg_monetary   = mean(monetary),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_clv))

datatable(segment_stats %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 2))),
  caption = "CLV Metrics by Segment",
  options = list(pageLength = 10))
```
